title: Air Fryer Lemon Pepper Tilapia with Roasted Sweet Potato & Dressed Greens
description: Light, high-protein tilapia with crispy lemon pepper crust, paired with roasted sweet potato and a properly dressed salad with pepita crunch.
ingredients: 2 cups arugula or kale (or spring mix)
1 tbsp extra virgin olive oil (for dressing)
instructions: Toss 300g cubed sweet potato with 1 tsp canola oil and 1/4 tsp salt. Air fry at 400°F for 12-15 minutes, shaking basket halfway, until tender and crisp at edges.
Pat 2 tilapia fillets dry. Brush each with 1 tbsp extra virgin olive oil total, then squeeze 3 tbsp lemon juice over both fillets.
Sprinkle 1 tbsp lemon pepper seasoning evenly over both fillets, pressing gently to adhere.
Once sweet potatoes have about 8 minutes left, add tilapia to air fryer basket in a single layer. Air fry at 400°F for 8-10 minutes, until fish flakes easily and internal temp hits 145°F.
In a small bowl, whisk together 1 tbsp extra virgin olive oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1/2 tsp Dijon mustard, remaining 1/4 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp black pepper until emulsified.
Toss 2 cups greens with the dressing until evenly coated.
Top dressed greens with 1 tbsp salted pepitas.
Plate tilapia over dressed greens with sweet potato on the side.
notes: Pepitas are calorie-dense and fatty — 1 tbsp is the right call here, not a handful. Salted pepitas also mean you can ease back on added salt elsewhere if sodium matters to you. Dijon helps emulsify the dressing so it actually clings to the leaves instead of pooling.
